title: Fix unauthorized upload error in production

requirements.text: Fix the 'Unauthorized: Only logged-in users can upload files' error that occurs when uploading files in production. The backend authentication guard for file upload operations must correctly support both anonymous and authenticated uploads, or the frontend must ensure the actor is properly initialized with the authenticated identity before attempting uploads. Investigate whether the backend is rejecting calls due to a misconfigured principal check, and whether the frontend is passing the correct authenticated actor during the upload flow.

quotes: I am getting this error Unauthorized: Only logged-in users can upload files in production version when uploading.

acceptanceCriteria: A logged-in user can successfully upload files without receiving an Unauthorized error
If anonymous uploads are intended to be allowed, the backend access check is updated to permit them
If uploads require authentication, the frontend correctly passes the authenticated actor and prompts login before attempting upload
The upload flow works end-to-end in production without authorization errors

constraints: Do not break existing paste creation, viewing, or history features
Do not remove or weaken authentication on endpoints that are intentionally restricted to logged-in users
nonGoals: Redesigning the authentication system or switching away from Internet Identity
Adding new upload features or changing file size limits
Fixing unrelated paste retrieval or paste-not-found issues
